nodes.label: 2c plasticity vs drift + depth-degradation-persists — digits
nodes.rationale: Two panels (n=3). Left: held-out accuracy vs read-layer plasticity rho (0=frozen..1=fast) is nearly flat (~0.905-0.923, peak at rho=0.3) while tap-drift (red dotted, right axis) rises monotonically 0.175->0.26 — 'slow read-layers marginally best with lower drift'. Right: linear-probe accuracy by SCFF layer for rho in {0,0.1,0.3,1.0} — all four curves collapse onto the same falling line (0.90->~0.76): depth-degradation is rho-invariant, i.e. plasticity is a drift fix, not a depth fix. Embedded verbatim in phase1-report.md.
